title: Label, relate, solve: geometry and trigonometry

objectives.statement: Calculate and scale lengths, perimeters, areas, and volumes while tracking the power of the linear scale factor.
Use the Pythagorean relation and right-triangle trigonometric ratios to solve labeled triangles and check bounds.
Convert between degrees and radians and interpret sine and cosine as unit-circle coordinates.
Use inverse trigonometric functions to recover principal angles and interpret them within a stated geometric quadrant or domain.

scenes.title: Perpendicular lengths do not simply add
scenes.contentHtml:
Perpendicular lengths do not simply add

A fictional locator reports an object 12 units east and 5 units north of the origin. Its software reports a straight-line distance of 17 units.

The components are perpendicular, so they form the legs of a right triangle. The distance dd is

d=122+52=169=13,d=\sqrt{12^2+5^2}=\sqrt{169}=13,

not 12+512+5. The hypotenuse must be longer than either leg but shorter than their sum; 13 passes both bounds.

This lesson uses a four-part geometric audit: label the quantities and angle units, choose a relation that matches the shape, solve, then check lengths, signs, scale, and units.

Starting check

  • If every length doubles, by what factor does area change?
  • Is 180∘180^\circ equal to π\pi radians or 2π2\pi radians?
  • In a right triangle, can a leg be longer than the hypotenuse?

If the spatial description is difficult, list the points and perpendicular relationships in text before writing an equation.

Source Note
claims claim-trig-functions, claim-angle-conversionsources source-nist-dlmf-trig, source-nist-si-angle

NIST records define trigonometric functions and the degree-radian conversion. The locator and Pythagorean audit are original.

Accessibility and alternatives

Every essential shape is represented with named points, perpendicular relationships, coordinates, and equations. No task requires seeing a diagram, judging an angle visually, using color, or dragging labels.

scenes.title: Connect shape, scale, and angle
scenes.contentHtml:
Connect shape, scale, and angle

Geometry relates measurements to structure. If a shape is enlarged by linear factor kk, corresponding lengths scale by kk, areas by k2k^2, and volumes by k3k^3.

For a right triangle with legs a,ba,b and hypotenuse cc,

a2+b2=c2.a^2+b^2=c^2.

Relative to an acute angle θ\theta:

sin⁡θ=oppositehypotenuse,cos⁡θ=adjacenthypotenuse,tan⁡θ=oppositeadjacent.\sin\theta=\frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}},\quad \cos\theta=\frac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}},\quad \tan\theta=\frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}}.

The labels “opposite” and “adjacent” depend on the chosen angle; the hypotenuse is always opposite the right angle.

Definition
id definition-radian

One radian is the angle subtending an arc whose length equals the circle radius. A full turn is 2π2\pi radians, so 180∘=π180^\circ=\pi radians and 1∘=π/1801^\circ=\pi/180 radians.

Unit-circle meaning

On a unit circle centered at the origin, the point reached by angle θ\theta measured counterclockwise from the positive horizontal axis has coordinates

(cos⁡θ,sin⁡θ).(\cos\theta,\sin\theta).

This extends sine and cosine beyond acute triangles and supplies signs by quadrant. It also gives sin2⁡θ+cos2⁡θ=1\sin^2\theta+\cos^2\theta=1 from the circle equation.

Recovering an angle

If a right triangle has opposite/adjacent ratio rr, then θ=arctan⁡(r)\theta=\arctan(r) returns a principal angle. Context must still identify the intended quadrant. Calculator angle mode must match the requested unit.

Source Note
claims claim-trig-functions, claim-angle-conversion, claim-unit-circlesources source-nist-dlmf-trig, source-nist-si-angle

NIST supports trigonometric definitions/relations and exact degree-radian conversion. The geometric scaling and accessible explanations are original mathematical synthesis.

scenes.title: Recover a distance and direction
scenes.contentHtml:
Recover a distance and direction

A fictional beacon lies at coordinate (9,12)(9,12) relative to an origin. Find its straight-line distance and its counterclockwise angle from the positive horizontal axis.

Worked Example
id worked-example-beacon

1. Label. Horizontal leg 9, vertical leg 12, both positive; the point lies in the first quadrant.

2. Distance.

d=92+122=225=15.d=\sqrt{9^2+12^2}=\sqrt{225}=15.

3. Direction.

tan⁡θ=129=43,\tan\theta=\frac{12}{9}=\frac43,

so θ=arctan⁡(4/3)≈53.13∘≈0.9273 rad\theta=\arctan(4/3)\approx53.13^\circ\approx0.9273\text{ rad}.

4. Check ratios. cos⁡θ=9/15=0.6\cos\theta=9/15=0.6 and sin⁡θ=12/15=0.8\sin\theta=12/15=0.8; 0.62+0.82=10.6^2+0.8^2=1.

5. Check bounds and quadrant. The distance lies between 12 and 21; both coordinates are positive, so the acute first-quadrant angle is consistent.

Check
id check-angle-mode

If a calculator returns approximately 0.9273, why is reporting 0.9273∘0.9273^\circ wrong?

The value is in radians. It must be labeled as radians or converted to about 53.13∘53.13^\circ.

Source Note
claims claim-trig-functions, claim-angle-conversion, claim-unit-circlesources source-nist-dlmf-trig, source-nist-si-angle

The beacon, values, solution, and checks are original applications of the sourced relations.

scenes.title: Inverse sine is not reciprocal sine
scenes.contentHtml:
Inverse sine is not reciprocal sine

Misconception
id misconception-inverse-reciprocal

The notation sin−1⁡(x)\sin^{-1}(x) often means the inverse function arcsin⁡(x)\arcsin(x): it returns an angle whose sine is xx. It does not mean 1/sin⁡(x)1/\sin(x), which is the reciprocal function cosecant.

For example,

arcsin⁡(0.5)=30∘=π/6,\arcsin(0.5)=30^\circ=\pi/6,

whereas 1/sin⁡(0.5)1/\sin(0.5) treats 0.5 as an angle input and is a different calculation.

Write arcsin, arccos, or arctan when ambiguity matters. Then label the returned angle unit and use geometric context to select or reject a quadrant.

Source Note
claims claim-trig-functions, claim-inverse-trigsources source-nist-dlmf-trig

The distinction and numerical counterexample are original teaching expression based on standard function and inverse-function notation.

scenes.title: Choose the relation from the labels
scenes.contentHtml:
Choose the relation from the labels

1. Scale a shape

A rectangle's linear dimensions are multiplied by 1.5. What happens to its area?

Feedback: Area is multiplied by 1.52=2.251.5^2=2.25.

2. Complete a right triangle

The hypotenuse is 10 and one leg is 6. Find the other leg.

Feedback: 102−62=8\sqrt{10^2-6^2}=8. The leg is below the hypotenuse.

3. Convert angle units

Convert 135∘135^\circ to radians.

Feedback: 135(π/180)=3π/4135(\pi/180)=3\pi/4 radians.

4. Choose a ratio

Relative to angle θ\theta, the adjacent leg is 7 and hypotenuse is 25. Find θ\theta in degrees.

Feedback: cos⁡θ=7/25\cos\theta=7/25, so θ=arccos⁡(7/25)≈73.74∘\theta=\arccos(7/25)\approx73.74^\circ.

Recovery route

  • Wrong side labels: name the reference angle before opposite and adjacent.
  • Lengths added instead of squared: state whether they are collinear or perpendicular.
  • Area or volume scaled linearly: apply the scale factor once per dimension.
  • Inverse function confused with reciprocal: write arc notation explicitly.
  • Plausible digits but wrong angle: label degrees or radians before interpreting.
Source Note
claims claim-trig-functions, claim-angle-conversion, claim-inverse-trigsources source-nist-dlmf-trig, source-nist-si-angle

All practice, feedback, and recovery guidance are original.

scenes.title: Audit an angled span
scenes.contentHtml:
Audit an angled span

A fictional straight span has length 20 units and makes a 35∘35^\circ angle above the positive horizontal direction. Determine its horizontal and vertical extents, then reconstruct the length and angle from those extents.

Feedback after a complete attempt

The horizontal extent is adjacent to the angle:

x=20cos⁡35∘≈16.38.x=20\cos35^\circ\approx16.38.

The vertical extent is opposite:

y=20sin⁡35∘≈11.47.y=20\sin35^\circ\approx11.47.

Reconstruct length:

x2+y2≈20.\sqrt{x^2+y^2}\approx20.

Reconstruct direction:

arctan⁡(y/x)≈35∘.\arctan(y/x)\approx35^\circ.

Both extents are positive and smaller than the length, as a first-quadrant acute span requires. The context is fictional and the calculation is not engineering certification.

Source Note
claims claim-trig-functions, claim-angle-conversion, claim-unit-circle, claim-inverse-trigsources source-nist-dlmf-trig, source-nist-si-angle

The span, numbers, reconstruction, and boundary statement are original.

scenes.title: Rebuild the geometric audit
scenes.contentHtml:
Rebuild the geometric audit

  1. Name points, perpendicular relations, side labels, scale, and angle unit.
  2. Choose a relation: scaling power, Pythagorean equation, or trigonometric ratio.
  3. Solve symbolically before rounding.
  4. Use inverse trigonometry only after forming a valid ratio.
  5. Check triangle bounds, quadrant signs, unit-circle identity, and units.

Retrieval

  • Why does area scale with the square of a linear factor?
  • How do degrees convert to radians?
  • What are cosine and sine on the unit circle?
  • How does arcsin differ from reciprocal sine?

Complete assessment.json. If side choice fails, return to labeled ratios. If angle mode fails, redo the beacon conversion. If a visual sketch feels essential, replace it with coordinates, named sides, and perpendicular relationships before retrying.

Source Note
claims claim-trig-functions, claim-angle-conversion, claim-unit-circle, claim-inverse-trigsources source-nist-dlmf-trig, source-nist-si-angle

The synthesis uses sourced trigonometric and angle-unit relations through an original geometric-audit method.

authors.displayName: VSBDev — accountable author with Codex assistance

aiAssistance.disclosure: VSBDev (github:VSBDev) is accountable. OpenAI Codex, model gpt-5.6-sol, authored, researched, edited, and packaged this lesson in run d6ce8df3-939e-4724-8a5d-d7f48941a174. The digest covers discloseable material task instructions, not hidden system or provider instructions.

items.prompt: Every linear dimension of a solid is multiplied by 0.8. Enter the resulting volume factor.

answer.reasoning: Volume contains three independent length dimensions, so the linear scale factor is applied three times.

items.prompt: A right triangle has legs 7 and 24. Find the hypotenuse and give a bound check.

answer.correct: c = √(7² + 24²) = √625 = 25. It is longer than the larger leg 24 and shorter than 7 + 24 = 31.
answer.reasoning: Perpendicular legs combine through their squares, and triangle bounds independently test the result.

items.prompt: Convert 225° to exact radians and state its quadrant in standard position.

answer.correct: 225° × π/180° = 5π/4 radians. It lies in quadrant III.
answer.reasoning: The angle is 45° beyond 180°, placing both unit-circle coordinates negative.

answer.summary: At 150°, cosine is negative and sine is positive.
answer.reasoning: The reference angle is 30° in quadrant II, so the coordinate magnitudes are √3/2 and 1/2 with signs (-,+).

items.prompt: In a right triangle, the side opposite acute angle θ is 15 and the adjacent side is 8. Find θ in degrees to two decimals and verify the side ordering is plausible.

answer.correct: tan θ = 15/8, so θ = arctan(15/8) ≈ 61.93°. The opposite side exceeds the adjacent side, so an angle above 45° is plausible; the hypotenuse is 17 and exceeds both legs.
answer.reasoning: Tangent uses the two given legs; magnitude and the 8-15-17 triangle provide independent checks.
commonErrors: Using reciprocal tangent and obtaining the complementary angle
Failing to check the acute-angle range

items.prompt: A fictional right-triangular panel has hypotenuse 26 units and an acute angle of 40° between the hypotenuse and its adjacent leg. Find both legs and the area. A similar panel has every length multiplied by 1.5; predict and calculate its area. Reconstruct the original angle from your computed legs and include bounds and units checks.

answer.correct: Adjacent = 26cos40° ≈ 19.917; opposite = 26sin40° ≈ 16.712. Area = 0.5(19.917)(16.712) ≈ 166.43 square units. The similar panel's area factor is 1.5² = 2.25, so area ≈ 374.47 square units. Reconstructed angle arctan(16.712/19.917) ≈ 40°. Each leg is positive and below 26, and 19.917² + 16.712² ≈ 26².
answer.summary: Legs ≈ 19.92 and 16.71; area ≈ 166.43; scaled area ≈ 374.47 square units.
answer.reasoning: Cosine and sine resolve the hypotenuse relative to the named angle; triangular area uses both perpendicular legs; similar-area scaling squares the linear factor.
acceptableResponses: Numerical values within rounding tolerance of 0.1 with all geometric checks and square-unit labels
commonErrors: Using 1.5 rather than 2.25 for area scaling

criteria.description: Cubes the linear factor and obtains 0.512.
Checks against the larger leg and leg sum.
Selects cosine-negative, sine-positive coordinates with correct magnitudes.
Uses side ordering or the 8-15-17 relation as a check.
Calculates original area with square units.
Uses area factor 2.25 for the similar panel.
Checks Pythagorean relation, bounds, and units.

claims.statement: Sine, cosine, and tangent are related trigonometric functions, with tangent equal to sine divided by cosine where cosine is nonzero.
claims.scope: Real-angle trigonometry used for right triangles and unit-circle coordinates.
claims.uncertainty: Complex arguments and analytic continuation are outside scope.

claims.statement: A plane angle of one degree equals pi/180 radians, so a half turn is 180 degrees or pi radians.
claims.uncertainty: Rounding numerical angle values can slightly perturb reconstructed lengths.

claims.statement: For a real angle measured from the positive horizontal axis, cosine and sine give the horizontal and vertical coordinates on the unit circle, implying sine squared plus cosine squared equals one.
claims.scope: Standard unit-circle interpretation over real angles.
claims.uncertainty: Orientation conventions must be stated when a field measures angles from a different reference direction.

claims.statement: Inverse trigonometric functions return principal angles; geometric context and quadrant information are needed to interpret an intended direction beyond that principal range.
claims.scope: Real inverse sine, cosine, and tangent calculations in the lesson.
claims.uncertainty: Alternative software notation can use a superscript minus one ambiguously for inverse versus reciprocal functions.

terms.definition: The side opposite the right angle in a right triangle; it is the triangle's longest side.
A plane-angle unit defined by arc length divided by radius; a full turn is 2 pi radians.
For a right-triangle acute angle, opposite divided by hypotenuse; on the unit circle, the vertical coordinate.
For a right-triangle acute angle, adjacent divided by hypotenuse; on the unit circle, the horizontal coordinate.
For a right-triangle acute angle, opposite divided by adjacent; equivalently sine divided by cosine where defined.
A function such as arcsin, arccos, or arctan that returns a principal angle from a trigonometric ratio.
The multiplier applied to corresponding lengths; areas and volumes scale by its square and cube respectively.

Originality, evidence, and rights

The geometric-audit structure, locator opening, beacon example, inverse/reciprocal repair, angled-span transfer, all coordinates, values, feedback, recovery, and assessments are original. Every applied setting is fictional. NIST sources provide only standard trigonometric relations and the exact degree-radian conversion. No source expression, diagram, table, or example is copied or adapted.

NIST rights and public robots routes were checked before access on 2026-07-22. No agent restriction, login, paywall, or access-control barrier was found. No third-party assets, datasets, personal data, or patient material are included.

This mathematical lesson provides no clinical or engineering advice and makes no claim of review, approval, publication, measured effectiveness, credit, or competence.
